seo.title: Krabi Travel Guide: Everything You Need to Know (2026)
seo.metaDescription: Complete Krabi travel guide for 2026. 4-day itinerary, flights from Bangkok, Railay Beach tips, southern Thai food guide, budget breakdown, and transport advice.

intro: Krabi is where the Andaman coast delivers its most dramatic scenery — limestone karsts erupting from turquoise water, boat-access beaches, and jungle-covered mountains with hidden hot springs. Four days is the sweet spot: enough time for island hopping, temple climbing, jungle pools, and plenty of southern Thai food. This guide covers the practical details — getting there, getting around, what to eat, and how much to budget.

itinerary.title: 4 Days in Krabi: Islands, Temples & Jungle Pools

days.title: Ao Nang & Railay Beach
activities: Morning: Settle into your Ao Nang hotel, walk the beachfront road and orient yourself — find the longtail boat pier at the eastern end
Late morning: Take a longtail to Railay Beach (100 THB, 15 min) — explore Railay West swimming beach first
Lunch: Eat at one of the restaurants on Railay East — prices are higher than the mainland but the setting is worth it
Afternoon: Walk to Phra Nang Cave Beach (10 min from Railay West) — swim, explore the cave shrine, and enjoy one of Thailand's most beautiful beaches
Sunset: Head back to Ao Nang on the last regular longtail (~6 PM) or stay for sunset and charter back (800 THB)
Evening: Seafood dinner at one of the beachfront restaurants along Ao Nang's main road — grilled prawns are the star (300-600 THB)

days.title: 4 Islands Tour
activities: Full day: Join a longtail boat 4 Islands tour from Ao Nang pier (1,000-1,500 THB including lunch and snorkel gear)
Morning: Depart 9-10 AM — first stop Poda Island for white-sand beach time and snorkeling
Midday: Chicken Island and Tup Island — walk the connecting sandbar at low tide (check tide tables)
Lunch: Picnic lunch on Tup Island or Poda Island (provided by tour)
Afternoon: Final stop at Phra Nang Cave Beach for swimming and cave exploration
Evening: Return to Ao Nang around 4-5 PM — rest up, then head to the Ao Nang night bazaar for street food

days.title: Tiger Cave Temple, Emerald Pool & Hot Springs
activities: Early morning: Rent a scooter (200-300 THB/day) and head to Tiger Cave Temple (8 km from Krabi Town) — start the 1,237-step climb before 8 AM to avoid the heat
Mid-morning: Recover at the base temple complex — explore the cave shrines and monk meditation areas
Lunch: Stop in Krabi Town for a cheap local lunch — riverside restaurants serve excellent southern Thai curry (50-100 THB)
Afternoon: Drive to Emerald Pool (65 km, ~1.5 hrs) — swim in the crystal-clear emerald waters (400 THB entrance), then walk to the Blue Pool (1.4 km trail)
Late afternoon: Hot Spring Waterfall (15 min from Emerald Pool, 200 THB) — soak in the natural thermal pools surrounded by forest
Evening: Drive back to Ao Nang — if it's Friday or Saturday, detour through Krabi Town Walking Street for the night market

days.title: Rock Climbing or Koh Phi Phi Day Trip
activities: Option A — Rock Climbing: Half-day climbing course at Railay (1,000-2,000 THB with gear). No experience needed — instructors teach basics and you'll climb 4-5 routes on dramatic limestone cliffs
Option A afternoon: Relax on Railay West Beach, swim at Phra Nang, explore Tonsai Beach on foot
Option B — Koh Phi Phi: Full-day speedboat tour (1,500-3,000 THB) — Maya Bay, Pileh Lagoon, snorkeling, and Monkey Beach
Lunch: Included in Phi Phi tour or restaurants on Railay depending on choice
Afternoon: Last souvenir shopping on Ao Nang's main strip or final beach time
Evening: Farewell dinner — splurge on a seafood feast at an Ao Nang beachfront restaurant with sunset views

transport.fromBangkok: Krabi International Airport (KBV) has multiple daily flights from Bangkok — AirAsia, Nok Air, and Thai Lion Air from 1,500 THB one-way (1.5 hours). Airport to Ao Nang is 30 minutes by shared shuttle (150 THB) or private taxi (600 THB). Buses from Bangkok's Southern Bus Terminal take 10-12 hours (from 600 THB) — the overnight VIP bus saves a hotel night. Minivans from Phuket take 3 hours (300 THB). Ferries connect from Koh Phi Phi (1.5 hrs, 450 THB) and Koh Lanta (2 hrs, 400 THB).
localTransport: Longtail boats: The signature Krabi transport — 100 THB to Railay from Ao Nang, 1,000-1,500 THB for 4 Islands tour. Boats leave when full or charter privately
Songthaew: Shared pickup trucks run Ao Nang to Krabi Town for 60 THB (30 min). Service runs roughly 7 AM-6 PM
Scooter rental: 200-300 THB/day — essential for inland attractions (Tiger Cave Temple, Emerald Pool, Hot Springs). International driving permit technically required
Grab/Bolt: Works in Krabi Town and Ao Nang — fairer prices than tuk-tuks. Ao Nang to airport around 400 THB
Tuk-tuks: Available but negotiate before boarding — 300-500 THB for Ao Nang to Krabi Town. Use Grab when possible
Ferry connections: Koh Phi Phi (1.5 hrs, 450 THB), Koh Lanta (2 hrs, 400 THB), Phuket (2.5 hrs, 550 THB) — reduced service in monsoon season

mustTry: Massaman curry — southern Thailand's signature curry with potatoes, peanuts, and tender beef or chicken in rich coconut sauce (60-120 THB)
Yellow curry with fish — Krabi's local specialty, milder than massaman with fresh-caught fish and turmeric-rich sauce (60-100 THB)
Roti — Indian-influenced flatbread cooked on a griddle, served sweet (banana, egg, Nutella) or savory (with curry). Krabi does the best roti in southern Thailand (30-60 THB)
Grilled seafood — fresh prawns, squid, and fish grilled over charcoal at beachfront restaurants and night markets (100-400 THB per portion)
Pad thai with fresh shrimp — the classic done right with Andaman Sea prawns at street stalls (50-80 THB)
Mango sticky rice — sweet coconut-soaked sticky rice with ripe mango slices, the perfect dessert (60-80 THB)
foodAreas: Krabi Town Walking Street (Fri-Sun, 5-10 PM) — the best street food concentration in the province at genuine local prices
Ao Nang beachfront restaurants — seafood displayed on ice, choose your fish and have it cooked to order. Tourist-priced but atmospheric
Krabi Town morning market near Chao Fah Pier — cheap Thai breakfast and fresh produce (6-9 AM)
Ao Nang side streets (one block back from the beach road) — same food as beachfront at 30-40% less
tips: Southern Thai food is noticeably spicier than central Thai — ask for 'mai pet' (not spicy) if you can't handle heat
Krabi Town food is 40-60% cheaper than Ao Nang — the night market is worth the songthaew ride
Seafood restaurants price by weight (per 100g) — always ask for total weight and price before ordering
Roti stalls at the night market often have the longest queues — that's how you know they're good
Railay food is the most expensive in the province — everything comes by boat. Eat on the mainland when possible

etiquette: Cover shoulders and knees at Tiger Cave Temple — sarongs available to borrow at the entrance
Remove shoes before entering any temple building
Don't point your feet at Buddha images when sitting
Krabi province is predominantly Muslim in many areas — dress modestly away from tourist beaches
Tipping is not expected but appreciated — 20-50 THB for restaurant meals, 50-100 THB for boat drivers and guides
Stand during the national anthem played at 8 AM and 6 PM in public areas

budget.budget: 800-1,500 THB/day (~$22-42 USD): Krabi Town hostel 300-600 THB, street food meals 50-100 THB, songthaew to Ao Nang 60 THB, free activities (Tiger Cave Temple, Ao Nang Beach). Krabi is cheaper than Phuket and very budget-friendly if you base in Krabi Town and eat at markets.
budget.midRange: 2,500-5,000 THB/day (~$70-140 USD): Ao Nang mid-range hotel 1,500-3,500 THB, mix of street food and restaurants 400-800 THB, one tour or activity (4 Islands 1,000-1,500 THB, climbing 1,000-2,000 THB), scooter rental 200-300 THB. The sweet spot for most visitors.
budget.luxury: 8,000-25,000+ THB/day (~$224-700+ USD): Luxury resort at Klong Muang/Tubkaek/Railay 5,000-40,000 THB, fine dining 1,500-3,000 THB, private speedboat tours 5,000-10,000 THB, spa treatments 2,000-5,000 THB. Rayavadee and Ritz-Carlton deliver world-class luxury in a stunning natural setting.
